title: Clarify ingest vs retention SKUs + seatless dashboard options
preview:
From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@datagrove.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Cc: DataGrove Procurement <procurement@datagrove.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 08:12:00 -0800
Subject: Observability add-ons — ingest vs retention pricing?

Hi Rachel / Ben,

Thanks again for the demo yesterday — the dashboards looked great. Procurement asked me to get a clearer decomposition of the pricing for the observability add-ons before we can move forward (specifically retention and tracing storage). Two areas they want clarity on:

1) Ingest-metered vs retention-tiered SKUs — we need a worked example showing cost for our current profile (~120GB/day ingest, 30M traces/month, 45-day retention target for traces and logs).
2) Dashboards: is the advanced dashboard charged per-seat, per-project, or is there a seatless tier with a capacity/ingest floor? If seatless, how does that change costs and feature access?

If you can attach a simple matrix and a conservative pricing estimate we can take that to our CFO. Also, procurement wanted to know if you offer short-term pilot credits or a staged commit (3 months pilot -> annual).

Appreciate a compact breakdown — we can set a short call if needed.

Cheers,
Liam
Liam O'Connor
Head of Infra, DataGrove
liam.oconnor@datagrove.com

Attachments: none


From: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@datagrove.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Observability add-ons — ingest vs retention pricing?

Hi Liam — great to hear from you and glad the demo landed.

Short answer: both options are possible; which is better depends on predictability vs cost. I've attached a lightweight SKU matrix (retention_sku_matrix.xlsx) that walks through three models:

- Ingest-metered (pay-per-GB ingested; retention caps optionally applied) — best if you want no upfront commit and pay proportional to usage.
- Retention-tiered (committed retention capacity, e.g., 50TB/month for N days) — better unit economics at scale and simpler forecasting.
- Hybrid (ingest + retention credit buckets) — gives a floor for budgeting while protecting from spikes.

Key notes specific to your profile (120GB/day ingest, 30M traces/month):
- Ingest-metered estimate (45-day retention target): ~$13,400/month (conservative with low sampling assumptions).
- Retention-tiered estimate (committed 30TB/month retention bucket): ~$9,600/month with a 12-month commit.
- Hybrid pilot option: 3-month pilot with 40% pilot credits on retention and 20% on advanced dashboards, then roll to a 9-month prorated commit.

Dashboards: we do offer a seatless advanced dashboard tier that charges by project & ingest floor (recommended for platform teams). Seat pricing is available as an add-on where customers want per-user governance. Seatless tier still includes all features — only governance seats unlock admin controls.

I've kept the matrix conservative and included a diagram of trace storage lifecycle (diagram-trace-arch.png) to show where sampling and TTLs reduce cost.

If helpful, I can propose a brief 30-min call with Ben (pricing) and Kimberly (SLA/retention policy) tomorrow to walk through numbers and pick a commit path.

Best,
Rachel
Rachel Kim
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
rachel_kim@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0184

Attachments: retention_sku_matrix.xlsx, diagram-trace-arch.png


From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@datagrove.com>
Cc: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 13:40:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Observability add-ons — ingest vs retention pricing?

Liam — quick follow up on Rachel's breakdown. Two commercial levers we typically use to get to approval speed:

- Pilot credit structure: we can offer a 3-month pilot with 40% credit on retention buckets + 25% credit on advanced dashboard fees. This gives you time to validate dashboards and sample rates.
- Phased commit: start with a 6-month term at a reduced committed retention (50%-70% of target) with an option to step up once we see steady telemetry.

If you need explicit $ numbers to take to your CFO we can convert Rachel's estimates into a 3-line summary (pilot, 6-month phased commit, 12-month commit) and mark what changes if you enable a seat add-on for governance.

On the SLA side, Kimberly can confirm retention durability / egress expectations.

- Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Commercial, Redwood Inference
ben_carter@redwood.com

On Wed, Nov 18, 2026 at 11:05 AM Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Liam — great to hear from you and glad the demo landed.
> ... (see attached retention_sku_matrix.xlsx)


From: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@datagrove.com>
To: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, DataGrove Procurement <procurement@datagrove.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 09:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Observability add-ons — ingest vs retention pricing?

Thanks Rachel / Ben — this is helpful. A couple of clarifying asks from procurement and infra: 

1) Can you show a conservative worst-case for traces if our sampling stays at 100% for the first 60 days (we expect to tune down to ~10% after that)? That worst-case is what procurement will evaluate for budget.
2) For the seatless dashboard tier, does the ingest floor apply per project or per organization? Our org runs multiple projects under one account and procurement prefers per-account pricing to simplify billing.
3) Can you include egress assumptions (monthly restore / export) and whether those are included in the retention SKU or billed separately?

If possible, can you send a short one-page commercial summary (three scenarios with monthly $) by EOD our time tomorrow? We want to present to CFO on Monday.

Thanks,
Liam

-----Forwarded message-----
From: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 11:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Observability add-ons — ingest vs retention pricing?
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@datagrove.com>

(Forwarded for context)


From: Rachel Kim <rachel_kim@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam.oconnor@datagrove.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly_park@redwood.com>, DataGrove Procurement <procurement@datagrove.com>
Date: Fri, 20 Nov 2026 00:45:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Observability add-ons — ingest vs retention pricing? — commercial summary attached

Liam / team — thanks for the quick turn. Attached is a one-page commercial summary (redwood-proposal-v3.pdf) and the conservative worst-case numbers requested. TL;DR and agreed path: 

- We recommend a hybrid starting point: ingest-metered with a retention cap (seatless dashboard by account). This gives you the flexibility to tune sampling after the pilot while keeping a budget cap.
- Conservative worst-case (100% traces first 60 days) is shown in the attached; we price the worst-case but apply credits for observed lower sampling after month 3.
- Egress (monthly exports / restores) is listed as a separate line item in the proposal; small restores during the pilot are covered by the pilot credits.
- Commercial concession: 3-month pilot with 40% retention credit and 25% dashboard credit; option to convert to 12-month commit at a 20% discount on retention tiers.

Next steps: 1) If that sounds acceptable, I'll upload the proposal to your HubSpot record (HubSpot:23001234) and follow up with a short SOW. 2) We can lock in the pilot credits within 48 hours once procurement signs the acceptance email.

I've also included a scheduling link if you want a 20-min walk-through: https://cal.redwood.com/rachel/datagrove-proposal

Best,
Rachel Kim
Senior Sales Engineer, Redwood Inference
rachel_kim@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0184

Attachments: redwood-proposal-v3.pdf, retention_sku_matrix.xlsx

title: Type II intake: audit window & minimal evidence checklist — Acme
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Grant <olivia@acme.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Type II intake: audit window & minimal evidence checklist — Acme

Hi Olivia,

Hope you’re well — I’m Ben on the Redwood accounts team. We’re kicking off a procurement security review with Acme’s platform team and need to request your SOC 2 Type II report and a small set of supporting artifacts to complete our review. Key asks: 

- SOC 2 Type II report (audit period we’re targeting: 2026-01-01 to 2026-06-30)
- Bridge letter if the report is older / if controls changed since the period
- Minimal evidence: system description, control-owner roster, sample log snippets, retention policy, and a short control-matrix mapping (see attached checklist)

We can accept redacted artifacts for anything customer-sensitive. Preferred transfer methods: presigned S3, secure Drive share, or SFTP — I included a short transfer instructions doc for your team.

I attached a one-page checklist and a bridge-letter template (for auditors who prefer a short explanatory letter). If you can confirm availability and an expected delivery date, I’ll queue the rest of our review and loop in procurement as needed.

Thanks,

Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com | (415) 555-0197
Redwood Inference

Attachment: minimal-evidence-checklist.xlsx (xlsx, 34KB)
Attachment: redwood-bridging-letter-template.docx (docx, 12KB)

From: Olivia Grant <olivia@acme.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: acme-corp-security@acme.com
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 10:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Type II intake: audit window & minimal evidence checklist — Acme

Hi Ben,

Thanks for the clear list — we can provide our SOC 2 Type II report covering 2026-01-01 to 2026-06-30. A couple of quick notes:

- Our auditor requires redaction of customer PII before external distribution; we can provide a redacted PDF plus a bridge letter confirming no control changes since the period.
- For logs and telemetry, we typically provide timestamped snippets (redacted) and summary counts rather than full raw streams. Is that sufficient?
- Preferred transfer: presigned S3 links (secure), or we can stage on an SFTP box.

We’ll aim to get the redacted report and the checklist items uploaded by Friday (11/20). If you need anything in a specific format (CSV vs. PDF) call that out and I’ll shepherd it.

Best,
Olivia
Olivia Grant
Director, Security & Privacy — Acme Corporation
olivia@acme.com

On Tue, Nov 17, 2026 at 09:12 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Olivia,
> 
> Hope you’re well — I’m Ben on the Redwood accounts team. We’re kicking off a procurement security review...


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Grant <olivia@acme.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 08:30:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Type II intake: audit window & minimal evidence checklist — Acme

Thanks, Olivia — that plan works. To make it easier for your team I’ve pasted the minimal artifact list below and included a few one-line reply templates you can copy into your ticketing system. I’ve also attached the checklist and a short transfer instructions file.

Minimal artifacts (recommended, order-of-priority):
1) SOC 2 Type II report (PDF) — prefer signed copy from auditor
2) Bridge letter (if the SOC 2 is older than our target window or if there were control updates)
3) System and service description (1–2 pages)
4) Control matrix / mapping (controls -> evidence location)
5) Sample logs / telemetry snippets (redacted, include timestamps and anonymized IDs)
6) Retention & backup policy snapshot (policy PDF or export)
7) Admin/configuration snapshot (e.g., IAM roles list, firewall rules summary)
8) Contact for technical follow-ups (name/email)

One-line reply templates you can use: 
- "We will share a redacted PDF of the SOC 2 Type II report and a bridge letter via presigned S3 by 2026-11-20."
- "We will provide timestamped, redacted log snippets (CSV) and a control mapping document."

Transfer options we accept: presigned S3 (preferred), secure Drive share, SFTP. If you send presigned links please include expiry (min 7 days) and point us to any path prefixes.

HubSpot record for reference: https://hubspot.redwood.com/contacts/ACME-12345
Presigned upload instructions (short): see attached transfer-instructions.txt

Attachment: minimal-evidence-checklist.xlsx (xlsx, 34KB)
Attachment: transfer-instructions.txt (txt, 2KB)

If you prefer, we can also accept an auditor-signed bridge letter in lieu of some artifacts while you coordinate with your auditor. Let me know what’s easiest for Acme.

Thanks — talk soon.

Ben
Senior Sales Engineer — Redwood Inference



From: Olivia Grant <olivia@acme.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: acme-corp-security@acme.com
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 14:50:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Type II intake: audit window & minimal evidence checklist — Acme

Ben — quick confirmation: we’ll stage the redacted report and the artifacts on S3 and share presigned links by Friday (11/20). A couple quick clarifications: 

1) Audit period: you listed 2026-01-01 — 2026-06-30. Is that the exact window you need, or do you accept 2025-07-01 — 2026-06-30 (our fiscal semi-annual)?
2) For logs: we can provide 10–20 redacted rows per log source plus a short summary (counts by day). Would Redwood need larger samples for performance-related controls?

On Wed, Nov 18, 2026 at 08:30 AM Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com> wrote:
> Thanks, Olivia — that plan works. To make it easier for your team I’ve pasted the minimal artifact list...

Best,
Olivia


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Olivia Grant <olivia@acme.com>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 15:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Type II intake: audit window & minimal evidence checklist — Acme

Hi Olivia — appreciate the fast turnaround. To confirm: our procurement review target is the 2026-01-01 through 2026-06-30 audit window (inclusive). If your available SOC 2 covers the broader 2025-07-01 — 2026-06-30 period that’s acceptable; please include the auditor’s period notation in the PDF header.

Regarding logs: redacted rows (10–20 per source) plus daily counts is fine for our initial review. If engineering or our security team needs deeper telemetry we’ll flag a specific request later — for now the redacted snippets + control matrix will be sufficient.

If the signed report won’t be available before your upload, the bridge letter you mentioned will be sufficient to proceed. Please share presigned S3 links (expires >= 7 days) to: evidence@redwood-secure-upload (we monitor that address).

Doc links / quick refs: 
- HubSpot record: https://hubspot.redwood.com/contacts/ACME-12345
- Secure share staging (if used): https://drive.redwood.com/s/secure-share/acme-evidence-presigned

Next steps: you upload artifacts by 2026-11-20, we’ll confirm receipt and follow up with any clarifying questions within 48 hours. Thanks for coordinating this.

Best,
Ben
Senior Sales Engineer — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.com

Attachment: redwood-bridging-letter-template.docx (docx, 12KB)

title: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alice Nguyen <alice.nguyen@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN

Alice — thanks again for taking the time yesterday. As discussed, ACME is preparing to onboard its first private deployment and we want to align on a recommended connectivity pattern that balances latency, operational ownership, and auditability.

I attached a short comparison (redwood-connection-comparison-v2.pdf) but high level the three paths we typically see are: 
- VPC peering (cross-account peering) — low-latency, simple routing but requires both sides to accept peering and route coordination.
- AWS PrivateLink (interface endpoints) — strong isolation and per-service endpoints, easier cross-account access controls, but introduces SNAT/egress considerations depending on your architecture.
- Site-to-site VPN (IPSec) — fast to stand up, supports on-prem and cloud, but higher latency/maintenance and less scalable at high throughput.

Could you confirm any hard constraints from ACME's side (account separation, must-route-through-hub, TLS termination preferences, requirement for flow/audit logs retention)? Once we have that I'll loop in Kimberly and Jordan for the deployment checklist and a pilot plan.

Next step suggested: 60-minute technical workshop this week to pick a primary and fallback path, with roles documented for NetOps and Redwood. Is Thursday 11/19 10:00 PT available?

Thanks,
Ben
Ben Carter | Senior Solutions Engineer
Redwood Inference
attachments: redwood-connection-comparison-v2.pdf (15 pages), acme-network-requirements-20261116.xlsx (stub)


From: Alice Nguyen <alice.nguyen@acme-corp.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: netops@acme-corp.com
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 13:44:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN

Ben — thanks for the summary and the docs. Short answers / constraints from NetOps: 
- We cannot accept VPC peering from third-party provider accounts into our production account due to lateral movement policy (we maintain strict account tenancy).
- PrivateLink is acceptable, but we need clarification on who manages the interface endpoints (ACME or Redwood) and how endpoint ENIs are tagged for billing and audit.
- We have an on-prem data center that will need access to inference endpoints for a pilot; we prefer VPN for on-prem -> cloud unless PrivateLink + Transit Gateway is recommended.
- TLS: all traffic must be TLS-terminated at the application layer (no early TLS termination at network edge).
- Logging/retention: flow logs and connection metadata must be retained for 365 days.

Thursday 11/19 10:00 PT works for the workshop. Please include your network architect and an on-call NetOps engineer; I'll invite ours.

—Alice
Alice Nguyen
Head of Cloud Operations, ACME Corporation


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Alice Nguyen <alice.nguyen@acme-corp.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 08:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN

Alice — great notes, thanks. Looping in Kimberly (security & compliance) and Jordan (network architecture) to address the ENI/management questions and the TLS/logging items.

Kimberly / Jordan: ACME's constraints: no cross-account peering into prod, PrivateLink is preferred if we can ensure endpoint management meets audit tagging and 1-year retention of flow/connection logs. They also have an on-prem footprint and would like a recommended path for on-prem access during the pilot.

Agenda for Thursday (proposed):
- 10m: Introductions & constraints confirmation
- 20m: Option A: PrivateLink design (ownership model, endpoint lifecycle, tagging, egress behavior)
- 15m: Option B: VPN fallback for on-prem (throughput, HA, monitoring)
- 10m: Pilot plan + roll-out checklist + required artifacts for procurement (SOC2 addendum, architecture diagram)
- 5m: Action items and owners

Attachments: I re-attached our comparison and also a short checklist of artifacts ACME will need for an enterprise Private deployment.

Ben


From: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>
To: Alice Nguyen <alice.nguyen@acme-corp.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 07:15:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN

Alice / Ben / team — quick technical note ahead of the workshop to set expectations. TL;DR recommendation: primary = PrivateLink (interface endpoint) with Redwood managing the service endpoint; fallback = S2S VPN for on-prem access during the pilot. Rationale below.

Why PrivateLink (recommended):
- Cross-account isolation: works without peering and aligns with ACME policy.
- Security & audit: each endpoint ENI can be tagged and we can emit ENI-level flow logs and publish to ACME's centralized logging (we will coordinate IAM role assumptions and S3 export policy).
- Latency: single-digit to low-double-digit ms for most US regions vs peering which might be slightly lower but not allowed by policy.
- Operational model: Redwood can offer a managed endpoint model where we create the service in our control plane and ACME creates the interface endpoint in their VPC; we propose an automated Terraform module so both sides get idempotent, auditable config.

PrivateLink considerations / mitigations:
- Egress/NAT: If ACME needs outbound access to the public internet from the same subnet, we'll recommend explicit NAT routes or an egress proxy managed by ACME. We'll document SNAT interactions in the runbook.
- TLS termination: per requirement, TLS will terminate at the application (container) layer within Redwood; PrivateLink only carries encrypted traffic.
- Logging retention: Redwood will ship VPC Flow Logs and connection metadata to an ACME S3 bucket with lifecycle/retention policy set to 365 days. We'll include log delivery SLA in the SOW.
- Throughput limits: interface endpoints scale but we will provision tests; for high-volume workloads we'll add a parallel autoscale lane and provide a fallback via S2S VPN if line-rate requirements demand it.

S2S VPN as fallback/pilot path:
- Fast to stand up for on-prem testing. We recommend setting up a pair of HA tunnels into a Transit Gateway (or equivalent) that ACME already uses. This keeps the pilot simple and avoids touching production VPC controls. For production we still prefer PrivateLink for tenant isolation.

Pre-work for workshop (Jordan action items):
- Draft Terraform module snippets for the PrivateLink setup and share by EOD today.
- Provide a short checklist for NetOps (required IAM, S3 bucket policy, endpoint subnets, expected security groups).

We'll review these live on Thursday.

—Jordan
Jordan Blake | Network Architecture Lead
Redwood Inference


From: Alice Nguyen <alice.nguyen@acme-corp.com>
To: Jordan Blake <jordan.blake@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: netops@acme-corp.com, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 14:05:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Tenant network handoff: choosing between VPC peering, PrivateLink, and S2S VPN

Jordan / Ben — thanks for the pre-read. This aligns with our expectations. We like the idea of Redwood managing the service side and our NetOps owning the interface endpoints (with the Terraform module for reproducibility).

Action items we can take immediately:
- Provision S3 bucket with the following policy template (I'll share offline) and create a dedicated IAM role for Redwood ingestion.
- Identify two subnets for interface endpoints (private subnets) and send cidr/subnet IDs before the workshop.

I'll send calendar invites for the 11/19 10:00 PT workshop and include the NetOps engineer on-call. Looking forward to the Terraform snippets and the checklist.

—Alice

title: Supplier activation: PO formatting & issuance timeline
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@northridge.ai>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Supplier activation: PO formatting & issuance timeline

Hi Aisha —

Thanks for agreeing to kick off vendor activation for Northridge. To get procurement & finance ready on our side, please can you confirm the following items so we can generate the initial PO and complete vendor setup quickly:

1) Vendor profile & tax docs — please attach your W-9 (or relevant tax form) and primary vendor legal name/personal tax ID to match invoices.
2) Remit address and preferred invoice method (PDF via email, AP portal, EDI).
3) Billing contact (name + email) for PO-to-invoice routing.
4) Preferred PO line-item granularity — do you need SKU-level descriptions or is an aggregated license + support split acceptable? Examples below.
5) Payment terms you expect (Net 30/45/60).

I've attached our vendor_onboarding_form and a blank PO template to show the fields Finance requires (redwood_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf, redwood_po_template.xlsx). Our internal SLAs are: once vendor profile + tax docs are in the portal we typically issue the first PO within 3 business days (exceptions for new legal entities or custom contract terms).

Can you return the W-9 and confirm the remit address and preferred line-item format? If you can fill the onboarding form and return by EOD Thursday, we aim to have the initial PO out by Monday.

Thanks — looking forward to getting this live.

Best,
Ben Carter
Sr. Account Executive, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | (415) 555-0183

Attachments: redwood_vendor_onboarding_form.pdf, redwood_po_template.xlsx


From: Aisha Kapoor <aisha.kapoor@northridge.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Date: Tue, 17 Nov 2026 13:46:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Supplier activation: PO formatting & issuance timeline

Hi Ben —

Thanks for the clear checklist. We're ready to proceed. Quick answers below:  

• Attaching our W-9 and vendor ID export (northridge_w9.pdf, northridge_vendor_ids.csv).
• Remit address / invoice method: we prefer PDF invoices via email to ap@northridge.ai; remit address is listed on the W-9.
• Billing contact: Priya Mehta (priya.mehta@northridge.ai).
• Payment terms: Net 45 is our standard for new vendors; we can accept Net 30 for volume-based agreements.
• Line-item granularity: For software we prefer two lines — (1) Product license (SKU + qty + unit price) and (2) Annual support (separate line). Implementation/one-time services we prefer as separate line items. We can accept consolidated license lines if you pass through SKU metadata in the invoice.

We have no issue with your 3 business day issuance target once the vendor record is active. I've attached the W-9 and a CSV with our internal vendor IDs. Let me know if you need the legal entity name spelled out differently.

Best,
Aisha Kapoor
Head of Procurement, Northridge Analytics

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Marcus Lin <marcus.lin@redwood.ai>
Date: Thu, 19 Nov 2026 14:02:00 -0800
Subject: Internal: PO line-item structure guidance for vendor onboarding (Northridge)

Ben / Marcus —

Following Ben's thread with Northridge, a few internal notes for Finance & AP to follow when we create the PO and instruct the vendor on invoice formatting:  

Required line-item fields (must appear in the PO and be mirrored on invoices):
- item_code (SKU or internal code)
- description (clear text, include product name + any tier)
- quantity
- unit_price (USD)
- total_line_value
- start_date / end_date (for subscription lines)
- account_code (GL mapping)
- cost_center (if specific to the deal)
- tax_exempt flag (Y/N)

We should prefer the two-line approach Aisha recommended for product license + annual support. For implementation/one-time services, use a separate, clearly dated line with service period and attach the SOW number in the description. Invoice must include our PO number in the header; otherwise routing will be delayed.

If Northridge can provide SKU metadata on the invoice, AP will accept an aggregated license line as long as invoice attachments include the SKU breakout CSV. If they cannot, require separate SKU lines for items > $5,000.

I'm attaching the vendor intake checklist we use when handing to AP for new suppliers (vendor_intake_checklist.pdf). Ben — after Northridge confirms legal entity name and billing contact (Priya), Finance will kick off vendor creation and you can expect the PO within the 3 business day SLA. If PR/Legal raises any contract exceptions we'll escalate.

Laura Bennett
Head of Procurement Ops, Redwood Inference
laura.bennett@redwood.ai

Attachments: vendor_intake_checklist.pdf

title: Solstice POC — capacity alignment, success gates, and next steps
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@solstice.ai>, Eli Torres <eli.torres@solstice.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-18T15:02:00-08:00
Subject: Solstice POC — capacity alignment, success gates, and next steps

Thanks again for the conversation today — appreciate the transparency on the workload and the evaluation goals. This note captures the call recap, a proposed evaluation plan, and the capacity profile we think will give you an accurate read on latency/cost tradeoffs. Please confirm or flag changes and share the procurement contact so we can get the trial addendum started.

Recap / business goals
- Primary objective: validate inference economics for customer-facing summarization and retrieval flows at ~30 req/s expected peak, with p95 latency <1.5s for single-turn generation and tail robustness during batch traffic.
- Success criteria (candidate list):
  - Accuracy: end-to-end F1 on the holdout prompt set >= 0.78 (we'll run your labeled prompts).
  - Latency: p95 <= 1.5s for median prompt length (approx 400 tokens).
  - Cost: token-based unit cost under $0.006 per 1k output tokens in production-equivalent setup.
  - Reliability: no more than 1% 5xx errors over the evaluation window.

POC plan (proposed) — 3 weeks (flexible to your ask)
- Week 0 (kickoff): infra provisioning, SOW/addendum signed, test data ingest, baseline perf runs.
- Week 1 (baseline + functional): run steady-state traffic (30 req/s) across candidate model variants, collect metrics.
- Week 2 (stress + burst): runway testing with simulated spikes, evaluate autoscaling and cache effectiveness, run retrieval-heavy scenarios.
- Week 3 (wrap): joint review, decision gates, handoff plan for Dedicated or Hosted with burst allowances.

Capacity proposal (what we recommend to mimic production):
- Baseline reserved: 4 x A100-80GB (reserved for the POC window) — this covers steady-state and enables low-latency runs.
- Autoscale / burst: policy to allow scaling up to 8 GPUs for short spikes; we can configure burst caps and per-minute billing for extra GPUs.
- Workload model: mixed generative + retrieval; we recommend enabling KV prefix caching for repeated user sessions to reduce token spend.
- Expected throughput with this config: ~25–35 requests/sec with p95 <1.5s for the reference prompt distribution we discussed.

Ownership and next steps
- Please confirm: procurement contact + whether you prefer a 3 or 4 week POC window.
- If you approve, we will:
  1) Send finalized trial addendum (DocuSign) + capacity quote. (Attachment: poc_capacity_quote_20261118.pdf)
  2) Schedule infra kickoff with Vivek (infra scheduling) and a technical onboarding with Lauren (eval scripts & metrics).
  3) Deliver a runbook (attached) with test plan, data ingest steps, and metric dashboards. (Attachment: poc-runbook_v1.xlsx)

Attachments: poc-runbook_v1.xlsx (test plan), poc_capacity_quote_20261118.pdf (pricing & line items), network-diagram.pdf (network expectations for VPC).

Thanks — happy to hop on a 15m sync if you'd prefer to walk through the plan.

—
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555-0118


From: Samira Khan <samira@solstice.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Eli Torres <eli.torres@solstice.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Procurement <procurement@solstice.ai>
Date: 2026-11-18T18:37:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Solstice POC — capacity alignment, success gates, and next steps

Thanks Ben — this is a great summary. A few quick clarifications from our side: 

1) Trial length: procurement prefers a 4-week window so they can align test sign-off with our internal release calendar. Can Redwood support 4 weeks without changing the baseline pricing materially?
2) Compliance: we need written confirmation that data processed during the POC remains in the US region and is covered by Redwood's SOC 2 controls for the duration.
3) Spot/pre-emptible: do you support running burst capacity on spot instances (we can tolerate preemption for episodic load) — any difference in cost?

Procurement contact: procurement@solstice.ai (cc'd). They'll need the line-item pricing and an SOW/Trial Addendum to proceed.

We'll review the attachments and aim to get back by EOD Fri.

—
Samira Khan
Head of Product, Solstice Analytics
samira@solstice.ai


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Samira Khan <samira@solstice.ai>, Eli Torres <eli.torres@solstice.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Procurement <procurement@solstice.ai>
Date: 2026-11-18T20:05:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Solstice POC — capacity alignment, success gates, and next steps

Samira — quick answers inline and concrete next steps.

1) Trial length: we can do 4 weeks with the same baseline price for the reserved portion. Burst hours are billed separately as line-items in the quote (we'll mark expected burst hours based on the stress tests).

2) Compliance/residency: confirmed — POC infra will be provisioned in the us-west region and covered under our SOC 2 scope for the contracted window. I will ask our Security team to provide a short attestation PDF; Lauren, please drop the SOC2 attestation link in Slack with Solstice.

3) Spot/pre-emptible burst: yes — we can offer spot-backed burst pools which reduce burst-hour unit costs (~30% lower) but subject to preemption. For trial fidelity we recommend reserved + on-demand burst for critical stress tests and spot-backed for large, non-critical load runs.

Ownership / schedule
- Lauren Bishop: will own the eval harness, script execution, and success metric dashboards. (lauren.bishop@redwood.ai)
- Vivek Kulkarni: infra kickoff and GPU reservation scheduling. (vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai)

Planned timeline (if procurement signs the addendum by Tue 11/23):
- 11/24: infra kickoff (Vivek) + data ingest starts.
- 11/29: baseline perf/reporting checkpoint (Lauren).
- 12/06: stress/burst runs and joint review.
- 12/11: final decision/checklist and handoff.

Action items from Redwood today:
- Send DocuSign trial addendum and final capacity quote now (link below).
- Lauren to share the eval script checklist and dashboards.
- Vivek to reserve baseline GPUs and open burst capacity window.

DocuSign (trial addendum): https://docusign.com/solstice-poc-addendum/abc123
Quote attached: poc_capacity_quote_20261118.pdf
Runbook attached: poc-runbook_v1.xlsx

If that timeline works, we'll start the SOW/Rolling addendum flow and aim to have infra live on 11/24.

—
Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Connor <liam@healthsync.ai>, HealthSync Legal <legal@healthsync.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 15:20:00 -0800
Subject: Draft MAP: data handling playbook & audit checkpoints (timelines + owners)

Hi Liam / HealthSync legal team —

Thanks again for the call today. Per our discussion I attached a first-pass Mutual Action Plan focused on the data handling milestones you asked for (retention, logging/audit trails, PHI/PII constraints and verification). This note summarizes the open items and proposed owners so we can align on a target close date.

High-level milestones (proposed):
- Retention policy & enforcement matrix — target: 2027-01-15 — owner: HealthSync legal / ops (dependency: Product sign-off)
- Audit logging & retention of logs (access + token traces) — target: 2026-12-10 — owner: Redwood infra (Ben) — dependency: log destination & retention SLA
- PHI/PII processing boundary & redaction flow — target: 2026-12-22 — owner: HealthSync engineering + Redwood integration (Marissa)
- Evidence bundle for procurement (SOC2 artifacts, KMS statement, encryption configs) — target: 2026-12-05 — owner: Redwood (Ava)

Open questions we need from you to finalize dates/owners:
1) Who at HealthSync will be the retention policy approver (legal or compliance contact)?
2) Confirm preferred log storage (your S3 bucket vs Redwood-managed secure object store) — this affects time to deliver.
3) Any specific PHI categories that must be blocked vs redacted at ingestion?

Next steps I propose:
- You review the attached MAP_v1 and add comment in the Drive link by EOD Monday (2026-11-15).
- We schedule a 30-min handoff with your infra and legal for Thurs 11/18 to confirm the log destination and retention SLAs.

Attachments: MAP_DataHandling_Playbook_v1.xlsx (spreadsheet), Retention_matrix.pdf (matrix)

Thanks —
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference

title: Private deployment residency — escrow, regional topology & staged failover proposal
preview:
From: Anna Müller <anna@eu-tech.co>
To: aditya_rao@redwood.example.com
Cc: Satoshi Ito <ops+cloud@tokyo-retail.jp>
Date: 2026-11-12T09:03:00-05:00
Subject: Private deployment residency question

Hi Aditya,

Thanks for taking this on. We're lining up a pilot with Redwood and have two hard constraints from legal/compliance: EU-residency of all customer data (including logs/metrics/audit trails) and a desire to have APAC presence for latency/continuity — but only as a warm standby.

Two specific asks:
1) Can Redwood host a control plane and primary data stores in an EU-only VPC (customer-managed) while allowing a warm-standby in APAC that cannot be used for reads/writes except during an approved failover?
2) What does an \"escrow\" or escrow-like approach look like for metadata and audit logs (legal wants an auditable copy retained in EU and a separate encrypted mirror in APAC)?

I've attached our initial compliance questionnaire and a sketch of our topology. We also need rough RTO/RPO numbers for a warm failover.

Thanks — can we have an architecture review this week? I'm flexible.

Best,
Anna Müller
Head of Cloud Ops, EU-Tech
[Attachment: compliance-questionnaire.pdf (PDF)]


From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.example.com>
To: Anna Müller <anna@eu-tech.co>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.example.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.example.com>, Satoshi Ito <ops+cloud@tokyo-retail.jp>
Date: 2026-11-12T11:20:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Private deployment residency question

Anna — thanks, this is helpful context. Looping in Laura (solutions) and Marcus (infra) to validate an initial approach.

Short answers up front:
- Yes: we can run a customer-managed EU VPC for the control plane and primary stores.
- Warm standby in APAC is supported: cold-read-only replicas + KV cache warmers; failover gate controlled by your SRE + legal checklist.
- Escrow: we typically ship an encrypted, signed metadata bundle to an EU-resident key management container (KMS/HSM) and replicate a sealed copy to APAC; actual decryption keys remain EU-only unless your team authorizes key release.

I've attached a first-pass topology (eu-apac-topology-v1.pdf) with two candidate models (split-control vs mirrored-control). Laura/Marcus — could you review the attached and call out network prerequisites (peering, allowed egress destinations, policy for cross-border logging)? Also: legal asked about log retention/immutable copies — Laura, can you propose where we'd store immutable audit blobs (S3 in EU vs on-prem vault)?

Proposed next step: 30-min architecture sync (we can do Mon/Tue next week).

Cheers,
Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: eu-apac-topology-v1.pdf (PDF)]


From: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.example.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.example.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.example.com>
Cc: Anna Müller <anna@eu-tech.co>
Date: 2026-11-13T09:45:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Private deployment residency question

On Fri, Nov 12, 2026 at 11:20 AM Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.example.com> wrote:
> I've attached a first-pass topology (eu-apac-topology-v1.pdf) with two candidate models (split-control vs mirrored-control).

Thanks — reviewed the sketch and sketched a third hybrid approach in the attached draft. High level recommendation (short):

- Control plane & primary data: EU-only VPC (customer-managed). All sensitive artifacts (PII, logs, audit trails, KMS keys) live in EU accounts.
- APAC presence: warm-standby clusters with encrypted replicas. Replicas run with keys sealed (cannot be unlocked/decrypted without an EU-based key release event).
- Escrow pattern: produce periodic signed metadata bundles (shards + manifest + sig) stored in EU immutable store (S3 object lock + separate retention bucket). A second sealed copy is replicated to APAC for resilience but remains cryptographically sealed.
- Network: no direct cross-border writes. Use one-way replication channels (push only) from EU to APAC using TLS mutual auth. BGP/eBGP for failover is risky across providers — prefer DNS-based weighted failover + health checks with feature flags in app layer.
- Failover model: staged failover: (1) detection + verification, (2) legal approval & key unseal, (3) promotion of APAC replicas to primary for a bounded window, (4) controlled rollback plan. Estimated RPO: ~15 minutes for state replication (depends on topology), RTO: 30-90 minutes depending on manual approvals.

Questions for Marcus: can we ensure stable egress IPs from EU VPCs for compliance allowlisting? Also confirm whether we can run S3-compatible immutable buckets in EU with object-lock in your proposed infra.

I've attached the draft runbook and the network ACL matrix. If this looks good, Aditya — let's present an option matrix to Anna and pick the legal-approved flow.

--
Laura Bennett
Principal Solutions Architect, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: regional-failover-runbook-draft.md (MD)]
[Attachment: network-acls-and-egress-table.csv (CSV)]


From: Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.example.com>
To: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.example.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.example.com>
Cc: Anna Müller <anna@eu-tech.co>
Date: 2026-11-15T14:00:00-05:00
Subject: Re: Private deployment residency question — infra confirmation

Thanks — short confirmation and a few specifics: 

- Stable egress: yes, we can offer fixed egress IPs from customer-managed EU VPCs via NAT gateways or customer-managed egress appliances; will call out costs and HA topology in the final doc.
- Immutable storage: EU S3 with object-lock + separate retention account is feasible; we recommend a write-once retention bucket in the customer's EU account (we provide bootstrapping templates).
- Replication: we'll use a push model from EU -> APAC with mutual TLS, no cross-region write endpoints. Replication lag estimated < 2 minutes for metadata shards, ~15 minutes for larger payloads depending on bandwidth.
- Routing & failover: prefer DNS weighted routing + health checks; can automate partial failover via feature-flag/traffic-shift playbook. Full BGP failover is only for certain network partners and would add complexity and potential data egress risk.
- Security gating: key unseal requires a multi-signer approval (legal + ops + customer SRE). We can integrate with your KMS (customer-managed HSM) or provide an EU-resident managed HSM.

Runbook highlights (attached):
- Canary promotion steps + smoke tests (automatic): /health, token-generation, sample inference checks.
- Manual approval checklist: legal acknowledgment, incident ticket, timeframe, rollback window.
- Estimated RTO/RPO: target RPO=15m, RTO=45-90m (depends on approval speed).

Next steps I propose:
1) Lock topology option (split-control vs hybrid) — Laura to annotate which option meets legal.
2) Sign off on egress IP model and pull costs.
3) Schedule an architecture review with the customer and legal.

Marcus Lin
Staff Infrastructure Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: regional-failover-runbook-draft.md (updated)]


From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwood.example.com>
To: Anna Müller <anna@eu-tech.co>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura_bennett@redwood.example.com>, Marcus Lin <marcus_lin@redwood.example.com>, legal-procure@nordicbank.se
Date: 2026-11-18T16:45:00-08:00
Subject: Proposed approach & next steps — EU primary / APAC warm-standby

Anna — thanks again for the initial ask and for looping legal. Laura and Marcus validated a practical approach that balances legal constraints and operational readiness. Summary for your review: 

- Topology (recommended): Hybrid — EU control plane + primary stores in your EU VPC; APAC warm-standby clusters with sealed encrypted replicas; no cross-border writes.
- Escrow / immutable logs: signed metadata bundles stored in EU object-lock buckets; sealed replica in APAC for resilience. Customer-managed KMS in EU or Redwood-managed EU HSM are both options.
- Failover: staged process requiring legal + ops approval to unseal keys; DNS-weighted traffic shift; canary promotion with smoke tests.
- RPO / RTO targets: RPO ~15 minutes, RTO ~45-90 minutes depending on approval cadence.

Attachments: eu-apac-topology-v1.pdf, regional-failover-runbook-draft.md, network-acls-and-egress-table.csv. Also adding links to our internal runbook (https://redwood.atlassian.net/browse/INFRA-2314) and the draft HubSpot mutual action plan (https://app.hubspot.com/deal/NB-PILOT-2026-11).

Would you be available for a 30-minute review on Monday 2026-11-22 or Tuesday 2026-11-23? If legal wants to be present, happy to include them.

If this looks good, I can convert Laura's recommendation into a formal options matrix and a one-page compliance summary for your legal team.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
[Attachment: eu-apac-topology-v1.pdf (PDF)]
[Attachment: regional-failover-runbook-draft.md (MD)]
[Attachment: network-acls-and-egress-table.csv (CSV)]

Subject: Townhall — Crescent Hall deposit, PO & cost-center split

Hi team,

We're ready to move forward with the Portland Community Lab townhall on Jan 22. Crescent has sent the venue deposit invoice for $7,500 (attached). A few items I need support on so we can get the venue locked: 

- Request: approval to pay the $7,500 deposit and create either a PO or prepayment. Crescent requires deposit to hold the date.
- PCard note: our PCard limit for events is $5,000; this would need a temporary PCard exception or payment via PO/ACH.
- Cost split: recommend cost allocation 70% Field Events (GL 5100) / 30% Community Programs (GL 5012). Please confirm if that mapping works for finance.
- Vendor docs: Crescent requested a W9 and signed SOW; Lisa attached the invoice and said the SOW will be returned signed once payment path is confirmed.

Marissa / Ben — can one of you approve the spend routing and either (a) approve a PCard exception for a one-time $7.5k charge, or (b) issue PO- prefunded payment? If PO, please confirm PO number so I can inform Crescent.

Grace — FYI for logistics budgeting and for vendor ops (AV contact below).

Aisha

Aisha Rahman

<aisha@redwood.com>

title: MAP Handoff: Skyport Health - MAP & Onboarding Timeline
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Amy Zhou <amy.zhou@skyporthealth.com>, Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@skyporthealth.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: MAP Handoff: Skyport Health - MAP & Onboarding Timeline

Marissa / Lauren —

Congrats team, we closed Skyport Health this morning (signed SOW attached). Per our post-signature process I’m handing off the MAP and initial evaluation timeline to CS so that onboarding and the POC evaluation run smoothly. Key background below, then a short list of asks to get the MAP kickoff scheduled this week.

Quick summary
- Size: $240k ARR, 12-month term, Redwood Dedicated (regional US deployment).
- Primary use case: in-app summarization + compliance redaction for clinical notes.
- Evaluations: security questionnaire complete; Amy/IT signed off on SOC 2 scoping.

Attached: Skyport_MAP_v1.pdf (mutual action plan draft), signed_SOW_Skyport.pdf

Immediate asks
1) Marissa / Lauren — can you own the MAP kickoff and invite the Skyport stakeholders? They prefer Wed or Thu afternoon PST.
2) Rafael / Vivek — please join for the technical kickoff to cover deployment options and expected latency profiles. Rafael, can you prepare the short infra checklist (GPU/region requirements) for the call?
3) CS to propose internal owners for: onboarding PM, 30/60/90 success metrics owner, and escalation contact.

High-level MAP milestones (draft)
- Week 0: Kickoff + confirm stakeholder list (Sales -> CS handoff)
- Week 1–2: Environment provision & baseline integration (test keys, networking)
- Week 3–4: Validation runs, quality checks vs evaluation prompts
- Week 5: Production readiness review, cutover window planning

Skyport primary contacts
- Amy Zhou — Head of Clinical Ops (evaluation lead)
- Daniel Cho — Infrastructure/Platform (technical lead)

Ben
Ben Carter | Enterprise Account Executive
Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0168

Attachments: Skyport_MAP_v1.pdf, signed_SOW_Skyport.pdf


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>, Amy Zhou <amy.zhou@skyporthealth.com>, Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@skyporthealth.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 11:03:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP Handoff: Skyport Health - MAP & Onboarding Timeline

Thanks Ben — congrats to everyone on the close. I’ll take point for the MAP kickoff.

Proposed next steps from CS side
- Kickoff meeting: Thu, Nov 18 at 2:00pm PST — 60 minutes. Will cover goals, evaluation success criteria, timelines, and responsibilities.
- Internal owners: Lauren will be the onboarding PM; I’ll be overall CS owner for the account; Vivek will be escalation contact for integrations.
- Rafael: please bring the infra checklist. We’ll need a short slide on expected latency/throughput and any network requirements.

Can Amy / Daniel confirm the Thu time or suggest alternatives? Also, confirm the list of stakeholders who should attend (we expect Amy + Daniel + 2 product SMEs).

Marissa Cole | Senior Customer Success Manager
Redwood Inference
marissa.cole@redwood.ai | +1 617-555-0242


From: Amy Zhou <amy.zhou@skyporthealth.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Daniel Cho <daniel.cho@skyporthealth.com>, procurement@skyporthealth.com
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 13:18:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP Handoff: Skyport Health - MAP & Onboarding Timeline

Thanks Ben and Marissa — Thu at 2pm PST works for us. I’ll forward an internal invite with Amy (Clinical Ops), Daniel (Platform), and Sarah Li (Product). Procurement is looped for a separate billing onboarding call but they do not need to be on the technical MAP kickoff.

A couple quick asks from Skyport before the kickoff:
- Can Redwood confirm the list of test datasets we can use for evaluation and whether any de-identification guidance is required?
- Can you share a short runbook for test key provisioning and expected token usage during evaluation?

Looking forward to the kickoff.

— Amy
Amy Zhou | Head of Clinical Operations
Skyport Health
amy.zhou@skyporthealth.com | +1 415-555-0829


From: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
To: Amy Zhou <amy.zhou@skyporthealth.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rafael Mendes <rafael.mendes@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 18 Nov 2026 15:42:00 -0800
Subject: Re: MAP Handoff: Skyport Health - MAP & Onboarding Timeline

Great — thanks all. Quick prep notes ahead of Thu’s kickoff (attached to this thread):
- Owner matrix (attached in Skyport_MAP_v1.pdf) — I’ve populated draft owners for each milestone; CS will finalize during the call.
- Evaluation plan: Rafael, can you confirm the target token budget and whether we should enable prefix caching during baseline runs? We’ll keep the initial run small to validate quality and latency, then scale.
- Test data: Ben / Amy — please confirm 3 representative de-identified sample documents for the evaluation; if Skyport prefers Redwood to apply de-id tooling, we can provide guidance but need a sign-off.

Agenda for Thu (60min)
1) Introductions & roles (10m)
2) MAP walkthrough & milestone signups (20m)
3) Technical checklist & environment readiness (20m)
4) Next steps & owners (10m)

I’ll circulate calendar invite and a short pre-read (MAP + SOW + infra checklist). Marissa will run the call.

Lauren Bishop | Onboarding Program Manager
Redwood Inference
lauren.bishop@redwood.ai | +1 206-555-0911

The Redwood onboarding team

Alicia — attaching the consolidated packet per your list. Files are included as attachments and there is a read-only Drive folder for larger artifacts: https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1A2b3C-encryption-evidence

Attachments (linked / attached):
- rotation_schedule_2026.csv (redacted ticket ids)
- kms_access_matrix_2026.xlsx
- hsm_attestation_q3_2026.pdf (vendor-signed attestation; redacted assertion numbers)
- encryption_key_lifecycle_playbook.md (1.2 pages)

Short answers to common SIG items:
- At-rest: customer data is encrypted with per-customer envelope keys; envelope keys are rotated per schedule and wrapped by an HSM-backed KMS.
- In-transit: TLS 1.2+ with modern ciphers; internal RPCs use mTLS and are permitted to use short-lived session keys.
- Key rotation: automated monthly for ephemeral keys, on-demand for compromised keys, and annual scheduled rotation for root/keystore pairs.

If you need an attestation letter on company letterhead for the HSM statement, Vivek can provide that — suggest a 30-minute call tomorrow to walk through the artifacts if reviewers want context.

Ben Carter
Senior Sales Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwoodinference.com

title: Pilot economics + host comparison — follow-up & asks
preview:
From: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@medlytics.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-11-15T14:03:00-08:00
Subject: Pilot economics + host comparison — follow-up & asks

Hi Samir — thanks again for taking the demo earlier. Quick recap and a short list of asks so we can scope a short pilot and run a price/host comparison that maps to your economics: 

Recap
- Core ask: ingest clinical notes + patient messages to power triage/summaries at ~150M tokens/month at scale (you said 100–200M range).
- Pilot: 6–8 week pilot to validate quality and latency on a 10M token pilot sample.

Asks (to scope pricing precisely)
1) Confirm expected monthly token range (low / mid / high). From the call I have: 30M / 150M / 300M — please correct.
2) Target budget range for inference (monthly or $/1M tokens). If you have an internal target $/1M token, that helps us tune proposal.
3) Current vendor pricing (OpenAI, Anthropic, any other host) or blended bill you see today. If you have a recent invoice snapshot that would be ideal.
4) Any compliance or VPC/on-prem constraints that would affect pricing (e.g., must run in your AWS account).

Why we ask: we want to map an apples-to-apples effective $/1M tokens across alternatives (hosted OpenAI/Anthropic, hoster-managed open-models, and Redwood options with caching/batching). Small differences in per-token and caching assumptions change the unit economics materially at your scale.

If you can reply with items 1–3 this week we’ll: 1) run a short infra + cost model, 2) propose a pilot rate card (tiered commit + pilot credit), and 3) include a benchmark sheet comparing OpenAI/Anthropic/hosted open-model assumptions.

Thanks — I’ll tentatively block time for a scoping call late next week unless you prefer earlier.

Camila Reyes
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
camila@redwood.com | +1 (415) 555-0192

---
Attachment stubs referenced: medlytics_volume_estimates.xlsx (requested)

From: Samir Patel <samir.patel@medlytics.com>
To: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>
Cc: 
Date: 2026-11-16T10:21:00-08:00
Subject: Re: Pilot economics + host comparison — follow-up & asks

Camila — great follow-up. Answers inline.

1) Monthly token ranges (estimated)
- Low: 25–40M
- Mid: ~150M (most likely)
- High: up to ~320M (peak growth scenario in 12–18mo)

2) Target budget range
- leadership is comfortable with an initial target of $400–$700 per 1M tokens for a production-grade solution (including caching/latency guarantees). If we can get to <$500/1M with similar quality + reliability that would be ideal.

3) Current provider pricing (approx, blended)
- OpenAI (chat/generation): blended effective ~$1,100 / 1M tokens for our mix (GPT-4/4o calls + higher context windows)
- Anthropic: roughly $900–$1,000 / 1M for comparable endpoints
- Internal experiments on managed open-hosts (self-hosted or partner-host) indicate infra-only marginal costs that could run $300–$450 / 1M if well-optimized (but ops + reliability risk to us).

Pilot scope: we can commit to a 10M token pilot within 30 days. I attached our internal volume estimates (medlytics_volume_estimates.xlsx).

Constraints: we prefer vendor-hosted for the pilot but will evaluate private/VPC in parallel for later phases. SOC 2 is required but not a blocker for pilot.

Thanks,
Samir Patel
VP Product, MedLytics
samir.patel@medlytics.com

---
On Sun, Nov 15, 2026 at 2:03 PM Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com> wrote:
> Hi Samir — thanks again for taking the demo earlier. Quick recap and a short list of asks so we can scope a short pilot and run a price/host comparison that maps to your economics: ...

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@medlytics.com>
Cc: Camila Reyes <camila@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-17T09:12:00-08:00
Subject: RE: Pilot economics + host comparison — follow-up & asks

Samir — appreciate the detail. Quick read of the numbers and a suggested path forward from our side.

Quick unit-econ read (back-of-envelope)
- If your blended today is $1,100/1M on OpenAI and roughly $900–1,000/1M on Anthropic, the gap to a self-hosted open-model is large but carries operational risk.
- With Redwood's caching/batching and routing to quantized open variants for non-sensitive traffic, we estimate an effective reduction of 20–35% off per-token raw model spend vs naive host calls. That means an achievable effective $/1M in the $450–750 band depending on commit and model mix.

Suggested commercial structure
1) 6–8 week pilot: 10M tokens at $450 / 1M (pilot rate) + engineering support to configure prefix caching and KV cache for session-heavy flows.
2) Post-pilot: if you commit to a 12-month reserved capacity ladder (thresholds 50/150/300M), pricing steps to $375/1M (50M), $325/1M (150M), $295/1M (300M) with SLA and higher support tier. These are illustrative — final depends on model choice & quantization level.
3) Benchmarking deliverable: we’ll run the same prompt set against OpenAI, Anthropic, and an open-model host (quantized) and produce an apples-to-apples cost + latency + quality sheet.

Attachments: redwood-pricing-slate.pdf (pilot + reserved ladder examples, model-mix assumptions).

Next steps we propose
- Schedule scoping session to lock pilot dataset and success criteria (I can join Friday Nov 20).
- Camila to share HubSpot record and any SSO/VPC constraints.

If that aligns I’ll draft a short pilot SOW and an initial quote.

Ben Carter
Senior Sales Lead | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com | +1 (650) 555-0147

---
On Mon, Nov 16, 2026 at 10:21 AM Samir Patel <samir.patel@medlytics.com> wrote:
> Camila — great follow-up. Answers inline. ...

To: Samir Patel <samir.patel@medlytics.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-11-18T10:12:00-08:00

1) Pilot pricing: Ben’s suggested $450 / 1M for a 10M token pilot sounds good as a starting point — Samir, can you confirm internally and we’ll turn that into a short SOW?
2) Pilot dates: aim to start the pilot week of Nov 29; scoping call to lock dataset + success criteria on Friday Nov 20 @ 11:00 PT (I’ll send a calendar invite).
3) Deliverables before pilot:
   - MedLytics to share the sample prompt set + medlytics_volume_estimates.xlsx (Samir attached — thanks).
   - Redwood to deliver a benchmarking sheet (OpenAI / Anthropic / host-quantized) and a pilot SOW. Redwoods’s initial pricing slate is attached.

HubSpot/Links: HS-MLX-34 (I’ve added the deal and next steps in HubSpot: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/contact/9876543).

title: Recap: FinTerra discovery — elevated access, retention, residency
preview:
From: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@finterra.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-15T13:12:00+01:00
Subject: Recap: FinTerra discovery — elevated access, retention, residency

Elena — thanks again for the productive call earlier. I wanted to summarize what we captured and confirm our immediate next steps so we can get your security team the evidence they need quickly.

Key items from call (high level):
- PII scope: customer names, email, financial account identifiers, transaction IDs, and derived metadata (risk scores).
- Data residency: production data must remain in the EU for FinTerra PII and transaction datasets.
- Retention: default retention for transaction records is 7 years for compliance; some analytics indices can be shorter.
- Logging & audit: every support or admin access must be logged with user, reason, timestamp, and TTL for log retention; you asked for an immutable audit export option.
- Access controls: RBAC + least-privilege for support; break-glass flow that requires an approval step and audit trail.
- Encryption & keys: customer-managed keys (BYOK) preferred for EU tenancy.

What we can do right away:
1) Share a concise SOC2 summary and our support access playbook (I attached a one-pager to this thread).
2) Draft a mutual action plan (MAP) that includes evidence list + timeline for the security workshop.
3) Confirm deployment options (hosted + EU-only residency vs Private/VPC) and where KMS can be customer-managed.

As next steps I propose:
- Security workshop w/ your secops + FinTerra engineering (30–45m) — proposed windows: Nov 19 or 20 (we can shift).
- We’ll send the MAP and evidence bundle by EOD tomorrow.

Are there any additional stakeholders we should include on the security call? Also, can you confirm whether the 7-year retention is required for raw transaction logs or only for reconciled records? That will help us scope the archival configuration.

Thanks,
Priyom
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
pri...@redwood.ai | +1 555-0193

---
Attachment stubs: redwood-soc2-summary.pdf (pdf), MAP-FinTerra-v1.xlsx (xls), support-breakglass-playbook.docx (docx)

From: Elena Rossi <elena@finterra.com>
To: Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
Cc: security@finterra.com <security@finterra.com>
Date: 2026-11-15T15:03:00+01:00
Subject: Re: Recap: FinTerra discovery — elevated access, retention, residency

Thanks, Priyom — useful recap. A few clarifications from our end so the MAP can be accurate:

1) Retention: the 7-year requirement applies to reconciled transaction records (legal/regulatory), not necessarily transient debug logs. We still need the ability to produce raw logs for investigations, but those can be archived and moved off hot storage after 90 days.

2) Break-glass expectations: when support needs elevated access we require a request from our on-call admin (email to security@finterra.com) + an automated ticket that includes justification, target tenant, and expiry. Access must be ephemeral and kicked off only after a named approver (our security engineer) signs off. All elevated sessions must produce an immutable session export.

3) Residency & KMS: we require EU-only key material for PII envelopes — customer-managed keys are preferred, and any key material must be stored in an EU KMS/HSM.

4) Audit schema: please include a sample schema for access logs (fields: actor_id, role, action, target_resource, request_id, timestamp, justification) and your retention SLA for both logs and session exports.

If you can include the sample schema and a proposed workshop agenda in the MAP that would be great. Proposed participants for the security workshop: myself, Marco (secops lead), and Lucia (compliance).

Elena R.
Head of Security & Compliance | FinTerra

On 2026-11-15 13:12, Priyom Das wrote:
> Elena — thanks again for the productive call earlier. I wanted to summarize what we captured and confirm our immediate next steps so we can get your security team the evidence they need quickly.
> ...



From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>
To: Elena Rossi <elena@finterra.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Priyom Das <priyom@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-11-16T09:22:00+01:00
Subject: Re: Recap: FinTerra discovery — elevated access, retention, residency

Elena — appreciate the clarifications. Attaching a short evidence pack and a proposed MAP (v1) that includes the workshop agenda and the sample audit schema you asked for. Quick answers inline below, and we can deep-dive at the workshop.

- Retention: we can support a 7-year archived retention bucket for reconciled transaction records. Hot storage retention policies are configurable; raw debug logs can be rotated to an archive after 90 days per your request.

- Break-glass: our standard flow for hosted customers is: ephemeral elevated token issuance (time-limited), pre-approval by a named customer approver, mandatory justification field, and automatic session export to an append-only log. We can mirror your request for an email-based approval step that creates an approval ticket in your tracker; we'll work with your secops on the exact webhook flow.

- KMS: we support customer-managed keys (BYOK) with EU HSM options. For Private/VPC deployments we can provide a configuration that keeps key material within your tenant and under your KMS.

- Audit schema & SLA: attached is a sample audit schema (CSV) and our default retention SLA for audit logs. We also support immutable exports to a customer-owned storage endpoint for long-term archiving.

Proposed workshop slots: Nov 19 at 10:00–10:45 CET or Nov 20 at 15:00–15:45 CET. Please confirm which works for Marco and Lucia and we’ll send a calendar invite + attendee list.

Attachments: redwood-soc2-summary.pdf, support-breakglass-playbook.docx, MAP-FinTerra-v1.xlsx

Best,
Marissa Cole
Head of Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference

Thanks, Priyom — useful recap. A few clarifications from our end so the MAP can be accurate:

1) Retention: the 7-year requirement applies to reconciled transaction records (legal/regulatory), not necessarily transient debug logs. We still need the ability to produce raw logs for investigations, but those can be archived and moved off hot storage after 90 days.

2) Break-glass expectations: when support needs elevated access we require a request from our on-call admin (email to security@finterra.com) + an automated ticket that includes justification, target tenant, and expiry. 3) Residency & KMS: we require EU-only key material for PII envelopes — customer-managed keys are preferred, and any key material must be stored in an EU KMS/HSM.

title: Upgrade pathway: hosted API burst quota and tier alignment
preview:
From: Tom Harrington <tom.harrington@parcelworks.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Aisha Patel <aisha.patel@parcelworks.com>
Date: Tue, 10 Nov 2026 09:12:00 -0800
Subject: Re: usage spike and renewal window (ParcelWorks)

Ben —

We just finished an internal review of our Q4 roadmap and it looks like our feature rollout will double token consumption for November/December. Current commit is 18M tokens/month; we need guidance on preserving latency while handling short-term bursts (up to ~45M tokens/month peak). Procurement asked for a quick sketch of options so they can budget during renewal.

Can you send a recommended path for a short-term commit increase + burst pocket and an expected rate-limit profile (tokens/min) so we can see cost delta vs pay-as-you-go? Also include any SLA implications if we move to a higher tier.

Thanks,
Tom
VP Product, ParcelWorks



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Harrington <tom.harrington@parcelworks.com>
Cc: Aisha Patel <aisha.patel@parcelworks.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 11 Nov 2026 11:04:00 -0800
Subject: Re: usage spike and renewal window (ParcelWorks)

Tom —

Thanks for the heads-up. Attaching a short summary and a token runbook (see ParcelWorks_usage_q3.csv and Redwood_upgrade_summary_v1.pdf). High level recommendation:

- Short-term path: move from current hosted commit of 18M -> 40M tokens/month for a 3-month bridge (Nov–Jan) to cover rollout, with a 10M-token burst pocket to handle traffic variability.
- Tier changes: bump to Tier 3 pricing band (reduced per-token unit cost at higher commit).
- Rate limits: suggest 120k tokens/min sustained per account with burst allowance to 220k tokens/min for short windows; we can shape at the gateway to protect latency.
- SLA: higher commit + reserved capacity qualifies for 99.9% latency SLO target (p95) and priority routing.

Estimated delta (rough): incremental commit cost + reduced unit price results in ~24% higher monthly spend versus current in-exchange of overage/PAYG, but gives predictable unit economics and lower p95 latency risk. Exact numbers in the attached summary.

Next steps: if Procurement wants a formal SOW and pricing table we can draft and loop in Legal (Laura). If this works, Monica can schedule an ops review with our Onboarding team for traffic shaping and KV cache priming.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference



From: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 08:15:00 -0800
Subject: Fwd: usage spike and renewal window (ParcelWorks)

Forwarding Tom's ask. Two operational notes below (please reply inline):

1) Cache warm-up: recommend pre-populating prefix cache for expected high-volume endpoints. Will need a sample of top-20 prompts / popular sequences.
2) Autoscale guardrails: suggest a staged scale to avoid cold-starts — reserve a pool across 2 AZs with priority routing for ParcelWorks for the 3-month bridge.

Karthik — can you confirm whether Tier 3 already has the throughput lanes to support 220k tokens/min bursts? Also, what's the typical lead time for spinning the reserved capacity pool?

Monica
Customer Success Manager — Redwood Inference



From: Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>
To: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Thu, 12 Nov 2026 10:21:00 -0800
Subject: Re: Fwd: usage spike and renewal window (ParcelWorks)

Monica —

Confirmed: Tier 3 supports sustained throughput at the levels Ben suggested, but 220k tokens/min bursts will need a short-lived burst pool (we can provision ephemeral capacity within 24–36 hours if requested). Lead time for a reserved capacity pool (multi-AZ + redundancy) is ~7–10 business days to guarantee capacity and SLA.

Operational caveat: if ParcelWorks drives sustained >150k tokens/min for long periods we recommend moving to a Dedicated reserved capacity contract to avoid unit-cost regressions and queueing during shared-hosted peak windows.

Marissa — please hold a draft SOW template for Ben to send if Procurement confirms.

Thanks,
Karthik
Head of Solutions — Redwood Inference



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Tom Harrington <tom.harrington@parcelworks.com>, Aisha Patel <aisha.patel@parcelworks.com>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik.iyer@redwoodinference.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Mon, 16 Nov 2026 09:30:00 -0800
Subject: Hosted upgrade proposal + draft SOW (ParcelWorks)

Tom / Aisha —

Per our conversations, attaching the draft SOW (ParcelWorks_SOW_v2.pdf) that covers: 3-month bridge commit (40M tokens/month), 10M burst pocket, Tier 3 pricing band adjustments, and priority routing with a 7–10 day provisioning lead time for reserved pool. Key commercial points:

- Monthly committed tokens: 40,000,000 (Nov–Jan)
- Burst pocket: 10,000,000 tokens (on-demand, billed at agreed burst rate)
- Rate limits: nominal 120k tokens/min; burst allowance 220k tokens/min during peak windows (gateway-enforced).
- SLA: priority routing with 99.9% p95 target; credits for extended downtime per standard SOW terms.

Laura from Legal is on the thread for contract redlines. For budgeting purposes, the estimated net change in monthly spend (after new unit discounts) is in the attached SOW summary.

If Procurement signs off, I can route this to DocuSign this week.

Best,
Ben

- Rate limits: suggest 120k tokens/min sustained per account with burst allowance to 220k tokens/min for short windows; we can shape at the gateway to protect latency.
- SLA: higher commit + reserved capacity qualifies for 99.9% latency SLO target (p95) and priority routing.

Estimated delta (rough): incremental commit cost + reduced unit price results in ~24% higher monthly spend versus current in-exchange of overage/PAYG, but gives predictable unit economics and lower p95 latency risk. Exact numbers in the attached summary.

Next steps: if Procurement wants a formal SOW and pricing table we can draft and loop in Legal (Laura). If this works, Monica can schedule an ops review with our Onboarding team for traffic shaping and KV cache priming.

Best,
Ben Carter
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
